plainspeak.doctor: Artificial General Intelligence, a type of AI that can perform any intellectual task as well as or better than a human.
plainspeak.lawyer: Advanced artificial intelligence capable of performing various cognitive tasks at the level of a human expert.
plainspeak.high-school: A highly advanced form of artificial intelligence that can do almost anything a person can do in terms of thinking and problem-solving.
plainspeak.kindergarten: Really smart computer that can think like a grown-up.

plainspeak.doctor: High-Bandwidth Memory, a type of memory used in AI chips to increase the speed and efficiency of data transfer.
plainspeak.lawyer: High-speed memory used in advanced computer chips for faster data processing.
plainspeak.high-school: Special kind of memory that helps computers work faster with big numbers and pictures.
plainspeak.kindergarten: Super fast toy box for computer brains.

plainspeak.doctor: Snapshots of application windows that can be attached to Codex threads, providing context for the work being done.
plainspeak.lawyer: Captured images of your computer screens that help explain what you are working on in Codex.
plainspeak.high-school: Pictures of what you are doing on your computer that you can send to help explain your project.
plainspeak.kindergarten: Photos of what you are looking at on the computer to show others.

plainspeak.doctor: A feature in the Hugging Face framework that allows for efficient model serving with reduced computational resources.
plainspeak.lawyer: A method to make AI models run faster and use less power without losing performance.
plainspeak.high-school: A way to make your computer learn things more quickly and use less battery.
plainspeak.kindergarten: A magic button that makes the computer learn faster and not get tired.

plainspeak.doctor: A technique for reducing the precision of model weights in machine learning models to save computational resources.
plainspeak.lawyer: A method to make AI models smaller and faster by using less data, but still work well.
plainspeak.high-school: A way to make computer brains use less space while still being smart.
plainspeak.kindergarten: Making the computer brain smaller so it uses less battery.

plainspeak.doctor: High-performance AI chips designed by Cerebras Systems for large-scale machine learning tasks.
plainspeak.lawyer: Advanced computer chips made to help with very complex and big data problems.
plainspeak.high-school: Special computer parts that are really good at solving hard math problems quickly.

plainspeak.kindergarten: A special way of making a computer understand both pictures and words at the same time.
plainspeak.high-school: An architecture that processes images and text together in one system, unlike usual methods where tasks are handed off separately.
plainspeak.lawyer: A novel computational framework designed to unify image and text processing within a single architectural model.
plainspeak.doctor: A multimodal neural network architecture that integrates the simultaneous processing of visual and textual data.

plainspeak.kindergarten: A way to make a computer learn new things without changing all its parts.
plainspeak.high-school: Low-Rank Adaptation, a technique for fine-tuning large language models with minimal parameters.
plainspeak.lawyer: A method of adapting pre-trained machine learning models by adding low-rank matrices to the existing weight matrices.
plainspeak.doctor: A model adaptation approach that allows for efficient fine-tuning of large neural networks using low-rank updates.

plainspeak.kindergarten: A way for a computer to choose the best helper when solving a problem.
plainspeak.high-school: A machine learning architecture where tasks are divided among multiple specialized models, each handling different parts of the workload.
plainspeak.lawyer: A computational model that employs a combination of specialized sub-models (experts) to handle various aspects of a task, optimizing performance and resource utilization.
plainspeak.doctor: An ensemble learning technique where a single model delegates tasks to multiple specialized sub-models based on their expertise.

plainspeak.kindergarten: A way for a computer to find similar things in a big list of information.
plainspeak.high-school: A method of searching through high-dimensional vector spaces to find items that are close to each other based on their similarity or distance metrics.
plainspeak.lawyer: A technique used in database and information retrieval systems to efficiently search for vectors (data points) in multi-dimensional space, often employed in recommendation engines and content-based filtering.
plainspeak.doctor: An algorithmic approach for querying large datasets of high-dimensional data by leveraging vector representations, enabling fast and accurate similarity searches.

plainspeak.kindergarten: A computer system that helps find the right information when needed.
plainspeak.high-school: Information retrieval systems designed to handle real-world, high-volume data requests efficiently and accurately, often used in enterprise search solutions or large-scale applications.
plainspeak.lawyer: Systems engineered for practical use in retrieving relevant information from vast databases, ensuring reliability and performance under operational conditions.
plainspeak.doctor: Advanced information retrieval frameworks optimized for production environments, capable of handling complex queries and high-throughput data access requirements.

plainspeak.kindergarten: A special way for computers to talk to each other.
plainspeak.high-school: An application programming interface (API) provided by a service that allows developers to interact with its functionalities programmatically, often used in web and software development.
plainspeak.lawyer: A set of protocols and routines for building and interacting with applications, enabling communication between different software systems or services.
plainspeak.doctor: A standardized interface through which external programs can access the features and data of a particular system or service.

plainspeak.kindergarten: A computer that helps organize problems.
plainspeak.high-school: An automated system designed to prioritize and categorize issues or bugs reported in software projects, assisting developers in managing their workload efficiently.
plainspeak.lawyer: A tool or service that automates the process of sorting and classifying issues or defects in a software development project, aiding in issue management and prioritization.
plainspeak.doctor: An intelligent system that analyzes and categorizes reported issues in software repositories, facilitating efficient triage and resolution.

plainspeak.kindergarten: A way to make a computer use less power when learning new things.
plainspeak.high-school: A technique combining quantization and mixed precision training in machine learning models, optimizing computational efficiency while maintaining model accuracy.
plainspeak.lawyer: An approach that integrates quantization methods with mixed-precision arithmetic to reduce the computational load of deep learning models without significantly compromising their performance.
plainspeak.doctor: A method for reducing the resource requirements of neural networks by employing a combination of quantization and mixed-precision training strategies, enhancing both efficiency and scalability.
